items.title: BKFF AI in Higher Education Fellowship: AI-enabled design ideation and robotic fabrication
items.summary: Georgia Tech profile of the Bill Kent Family Foundation fellowship cohort, including work embedding AI-driven design and robotic fabrication into architectural pedagogy, ethics, and studio instruction.

items.title: “Figural Assemblage” at Georgia Tech Library
items.summary: Installation video and prints in a group show context; work also listed for Korean Foundation Gallery, Seoul, and GT Library Media Bridge / Interactive Media Zone per CV.

items.title: Invited lecture — University of Kentucky
items.summary: Public lecture series and workshop on advanced digital modeling and AI image-based workflows (undergraduate architecture).

items.title: Solo exhibition: “Anomalies & Order: Grammars of Anonymous Architectures”
items.summary: Opening at the Mebane Gallery, UT Austin School of Architecture, in conjunction with the broader “Planetary Imaginaries” public programming season.

items.title: Weitzman news: “Robotic Fabrication and Architectural Design” and MSD-RAS research
items.summary: Penn Weitzman post highlighting the ORO Editions volume edited by Robert Stuart-Smith showcasing MSD-RAS design research; lab director contributed to the volume.

items.title: Georgia Tech student thank-you note (faculty impact)
items.summary: Recognition listed on CV: Thank You Note (Student Acknowledgement of Faculty Support and Impact), Georgia Institute of Technology, 2025.

items.title: Research profile — Georgia Institute of Technology
items.summary: Institutional research directory entry: computation, robotics, and machine learning in architecture; Georgia Tech School of Architecture.

items.title: Next Generation Indie Book Awards — Arts category (Purple Architecture)
items.summary: Edited volume Purple Architecture: Design in the Age of the Physical-Virtual Continuum (ETC Press) won in the Arts category; includes chapter “Faustian Aesthetics” co-authored with J. Billingsley.

items.title: CAAD Forum: “Grammar — Observations on Machine Learning”
items.summary: Center for American Architecture and Design forum at UT Austin on ML, 3D scanning, and architectural categorization, building on TRAITS / Innsbruck research themes.

items.title: Meet Patrick Danahy — Emerging Scholar in Design, UT Austin
items.summary: Faculty spotlight for the 2023–2025 lecturer appointment; background in computational design, robotics, and teaching innovation.

items.title: UT Austin welcomes new faculty cohort (2023–24)
items.summary: University-level announcement noting new School of Architecture hires including Emerging Scholars in Design.

items.title: TRAITS — Post digital neo baroque, University of Innsbruck
items.summary: Conference and exhibition context for “Machined Aesthetics” and related machined-assemblies research track (FWF-PEEK Postdigital Neobaroque project).

items.title: “Aesthetics of Prosthetics” — Pratt Institute (group exhibition)
items.summary: Exhibition coverage and related press on prosthetics, aesthetics, and computational design themes tied to exhibited student work.
